raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, a charge for {$1200.00} posted to my Citi Drive Card account for an alternator and related services/service plan purchased from XXXX XXXX in XXXXXXXX XXXX 

Soon after purchase, my vehicle developed major electrical issues ( flickering dash, speedometer, and other components ). The XXXX XXXX XXXX location closed permanently shortly thereafter, preventing me from returning to that store for resolution. I continued troubleshooting through other service providers. I was later referred to XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX after it was determined the issue was alternator-related. XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ) was unable to resolve the issue. I then purchased a different alternator from another source to be installed, which fixed the problem. 

When I retrieved my vehicle from XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ), I did not receive back the alternator I originally purchased. On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted the merchant in person and spoke with the store XXXX. The XXXX acknowledged that I was owed a refund but stated their store could not process it and that corporate would have to correct it. 

Because the merchant did not provide the product I paid for ( the alternator was not returned ) and did not provide the promised refund, I filed a dispute with Citi in XXXX XXXX for {$1200.00}. Citi issued a provisional credit ; the account reflected - {$1200.00} and I received and cashed a check for {$1200.00} representing the credit balance. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, Citi reversed the credit and the {$1200.00} balance reappeared as owed. Citi did not provide me a clear written denial reason or a description of the evidence used to make this decision. I requested details and supporting documentation ( including what information the merchant provided ), but Citi would not provide a meaningful explanation or documentation. 

I am requesting that Citi : ( 1 ) provide a written explanation for the reversal/denial and all documentation relied upon ( including any merchant submissions ), ( 2 ) reconsider/review the dispute based on the facts ( defective/not as described and the merchant retained the alternator and did not refund ), and ( 3 ) treat the amount as disputed and pause collection/negative credit reporting while the matter is reviewed.
